title: Central Package Management needs a single package source or a source mapping
explanation: Multiple package sources are defined without a source mapping under Central Package Management. Fuse can supply an overlay NuGet.config with a packageSourceMapping passed explicitly on restore, never written into the repository.

title: The installed .NET SDK does not support the targeted version
explanation: The project targets an SDK band the machine does not have. Fuse can install the pinned SDK from global.json behind --allow-install; consent-gated because it changes the machine.

pattern: A compatible .NET SDK was not found
title: The SDK band pinned in global.json is not installed
explanation: global.json pins an SDK band the machine does not have, so the .NET host cannot select an SDK and fails before the build (distinct from NETSDK1045, which fires when a present SDK is too old for the target framework). Fuse can install the pinned band from global.json via the dotnet-install script into an isolated directory behind --allow-install; consent-gated because it changes the machine.

title: An MSBuild task failed to load or execute
explanation: Often a missing workload or SDK-band task assembly, which a workload install can fix (consent-gated). It can also be a repository-custom task, in which case Fuse classifies rather than remediates.

title: Ambiguous type reference in repository code
explanation: A compile error in the repository's own source (an ambiguous type). Fuse never edits repository files, so this is reported with its reason, not remediated.

title: Unrecognized compiler option in repository code
explanation: A legacy or repository-specific compiler option the current toolchain rejects. Fuse never edits repository files, so this is reported with its reason, not remediated.
